reviews.review_title: Got hit with a huge rock and loved it.
reviews.review_description:
Don’t believe the negative reviews you read. RUMBLE is known to have a very steep learning curve. It is difficult to play at first but after some practice you unlock the real game. Most beginners get upset about this and leave poor reviews, but if you ask anyone who has played the game for more than a few hours they will instantlyreccpmdong it because in my opinion it is the perfect game.
Some to know BEFORE YOU BUY:
This game as a steep skill curve, when you first learn a move, like summoning a rock, it is easy because the game guides you. When the guides are taken away it is easy to mess up.
This game is difficult to learn, it makes you feel like you need to train like a real earthbender and is very rewarding when you figure it out. 
If you see anything online or meet and advanced player you see what practice gets you, it is the most inspiring game I have played in that regard.
This game is super well polished. Despite being in beta everything is made with intention, you won’t find anything that feels out of place.

When I first got the game I played a couple games got absolutely destroyed so I thought I would never get the hang of this game and just refunded it, then a couple days later I seen TikTok’s saying that you just have to get used to the game and its mechanics I bought the game again I started practicing and learned now I’m addicted to the game, things to know is the game tracking isn’t bad it’s ur posing, the posing hitboxes are really big so ur just doing the pose wrong and in the beginning of the game ur gonna lose a lot you just have to get a higher belt then you will be able to compete with the other experienced people and you will find it addictive like me, overall a really fun and addictive game.

reviews.review_title: It could be so much more (CLOUD HOSTING)
reviews.review_description:
1. No Cloud Hosting.

My Wi-Fi speed is 500mbps (extremely fast), and even then, half the time I have a huge delay between my movements and the rocks emerging, due to the fact that this game is hosted on PLAYER DEVICES instead of cloud servers; meaning that every game is a gamble between no delay, or extreme delay. I understand that funding would have to be substantial, but this game has been on the market for years, and this is the issue of all issues preventing this game from exploding and taking over the Meta Store.

2. Move detection is severely lacking.

This game's move activation is extremely picky, more than it should be. I own this game on both Steam and Meta, and have been playing for years, and this is still an issue.

3. Marketing is bottlenecking this game's popularity.

I don't think I've ever seen an ad for this game on YouTube, or any other platform. This game is too well developed for it to only a few hundred thousand players. But again, that might be because of no cloud hosting.

RUMBLE is to me the best VR game in existence. I have spent about 800 hours in this game on PC and Quest combined, and I can easily see myself playing this for at least the next 10 years.
This is the type of game made for VR. There is no possible way this could ever exist as a flatscreen game. It truly makes you exercise and has been amazing for my stamina and general health.
It also has an extremely friendly community and lots of tournaments, and might one day become a huge E-sports title. It also feels more like an actual martial arts than 'just a game'.

Some tips:
- RUMBLE is hard. It's learning curve is like climbing an overhanging cliff without any climbing experience. Then at the top you reach the highest rank, black belt/mountain. Only to realise that was only the tutorial and still have to climb all the way to Mount Everest.
- Muscle memory is not gained in a single day. Sleep is important in the learning process, that's when your brain makes new neural connections based on your practice. What feels near impossible today, is going to be easier tomorrow.
- The community is filled with teachers that love to assist you in this journey. Ask them ingame with holding A for push to talk, or set the mic to open in the settings. And join the discord communities for more help.
- It's not the tracking that is broken, it is your poses that are slightly off, even if you think you did the right pose. We all started overconfident, you need time to learn.
- Hand rotation is more important than hand position.
- Slow and smooth motions are way better for accuracy and consistency, which in turn helps you to speed up.
- Your head is also part of the pose and poses only work if you aim them forward towards your target, the opponent.
- At the matchmaker, play with 'all ranks', you learn faster from an experienced teacher than a fellow beginner. Finding low ranked beginners is way harder.
- The most active regions in order are US east, Europe, Australia and US west.

if your not the host of a game you pretty much might as well stop trying especially if your a low rank because not enough people are on to fight in the same rank so your forced to fight people with unbalanced skills and way better abilities i feel like this game needs a major update soon theres also skills that allow people to pick items up and throw them through all of your walls aka the only real defense you have in lower ranks it doesn't help that if you arent the host of the match then your moves will all be delayed its a pretty big issue in a game thats 1v1 only 
and theirs not any stamina or spam counters Especially when people pick up an unbreakable rock and swing it at you walking forward and you cant do much i still like the game but pls fix it

I have played the PCVR version for around 500 hours, I can say that this is the best £20/$25 I have ever spent, the community is nice and the gameplay is like no other VR game or flat screen game out there. The game is difficult to begin with but if you stick with it your skill will grow to levels you couldn't imagine you could.

Elements Divided I see compared to it, but the only similarity is that it's based on the elements. Do not be discouraged to play by RUMBLE only having 1 element because RUMBLE's depth in that 1 element is way higher than all of Elements Divided's elements combined.

Rumble is an awesome game there is just so much to do and learn, like flight, explode, chambering, waterbending, puppet masters, movement tech and that's not even close to everithing. I see a lot of reveiws that say the game is broken and that the moves don't work, that isn't true. In rumble you have to be precise with your moves and it just takes a bit of time getting used to. I agree that rumble isn't for everyone, but that doesn't mean it's a bad game.

Wanted to write something here, but i forgot what.




GO PLAY RUMBLE

Amazing game on a scale of 1/10 I would give it a 11/10 it might not be that fun until you can beat a 3000bp that’s when you feel like you’re fighting and not just “rock throw, rock jump, rock smash”

But if you have patience, the willingness to try new things and the ability to make things simpler you will find this an amazing game


Also the people complaining about the simplest of things only want rock throw, rock jump, rock smash, so don’t be them

That was my first thought when I first played this game 1.5 years ago, and you know what? It's still accurate. Playing RUMBLE feels like learning a new martial art (at least, it's similar to my karate experience). You struggle, you fight, you get tired, you try to build your muscle memory, and... You have fun. It's genuinely the most fun I've ever had playing a video game, it never gets old.

It's a hard game. I've been playing for a long time, and I still have a lot to learn, because the game has so much to offer. It takes dedication, and yes if you have a job (like many of us do), it's going to take months to get to a decent level. But we have teachers, sparring partners, and a lively community on discord. The community is probably the biggest strength of this game!

We often say "skill issue" as a joke because EVERYONE has a skill issue. This game requires you to learn a skill that no other game taught you, and it's frustrating at first. It feels like it's the game's fault when a move doesn't register, but it isn't, you just rotated your hand wrong. And if you keep training for just a few days, you start to understand, to *feel* it. You become one with rock. You are learning earthbending, and it's a martial art.

Also the Quest standalone release is so good, the devs did an awesome job on the optimization while keeping the game pretty. It even runs smoothly on Quest 2. True magic.
I first saw this game 2 or maybe 3 years ago on pcvr and thought it was amazing and i waited just to see they used a pcvr trailer for the standalone version of this game on the quest store

It feels like a real sport, repetition is King and you can get super creative with your play style and how you fight. Don't get discouraged too quickly, this game is hard... almost as hard as rocks. Jokes aside it takes a while to strike you poses consistently but follows the principle easy to learn but hard to master. The skill ceiling is as high as the sky you can fly to if you are good enough at manipulating your rocks :D

The game is all I wanted when I got a headset and to see that it came to standalone is SIMPLY LOVELY. This game has levels to how good you can get compared to other people even when you are at the highest rank. You will always have something to learn.

Honestly, I've been  playing VR games since 2012. A lot of just fun games, but this is the ultimate feel good game. I loved the avatar the last airbender show and now I do have the genuine feeling that I am bending earth! They have worked on the details of this game, little sound effects, amazing artistry visuals. You just know that the creators have their hart in this game. If you don't believe me, I highly encourage to try it out yourself!

Never played a game quite like this, and it's easily in my top 5 games of all time. 
It takes a lot of effort to learn, and is immensely rewarding.

reviews.review_title: Hands down my favourite VR game
reviews.review_description:
This game is amazing, it has so much depth due to being physics based that you can put thousands of hours into the game and still not know/perfect everything 

Rumble has no “characters” or “classes” to play as, but every player feels different to fight because there are so many ways to play. The community has made hundreds of moves to learn and master and this makes every fight feel fun and unique

I have 700 hours and am still improving and learning all the time, because this game takes a lot of practice. you need to be ready to loose a lot when you first pick this game up, I didn’t start winning fights until thousands of bp.

But don’t be discouraged, while this game is very difficult and takes a lot of practice, there are tons of people in the community discords (and in game) who are willing to help out new players and teach them things

Don’t be afraid to ask for help if you’re struggling, as chances are the community’s found a better way that they can teach you

Been an earthbender for almost a year now with 500+ hours, and all I can say is it's probably the best game I've picked up in terms of self-improvement and health. Hope to see more content next year, here's to another year of Rumble. 🥳

I was stuck in the dessert on sandstone, I had just run out of water and I was going to die. The only thing I had was a Meta quest, I thought it was the end, but then I learned how to earth bend and found my way to safety, I owe you guys my life
It’s perfect but I want structure skins

From the negative reviews that I have looked at, they seem to not like rumble due to the players and the purposefully hard to learn mechanics. One thing to note is that the game was released on pcvr 4 years ago, so using that knowledge you can guess that the players are mostly going to be old and good at the game. Overall, just a very peak game🥹

reviews.review_title: Rumble is a very fun game
reviews.review_description:
This is my favorite game but I’m biased 

A tip for if you want to stick with the game is to ask questions like almost all good players are more than happy to help

Im on most of the main discord and I specialize in flick (the flying around and stuff)

Also remember that rumble is a slow game it will take you a long time to “get good” just focus on doing what you like

I’ve spent a lot of time in vr, even having 100 hours in pc Rumble and this is still the best vr game I’ve ever played, and by best vr game, I mean the game that best shows what the medium can be.

Not that all other vr games are bad, many are truly incredible, but literally every other game has a pc equivalent. Shooters, rhythm games, chat rooms, even if vr changes the experience somewhat, there is still a pc equivalent. Most games make vr just another console to play the same stuff in a new way. Rumble is the only game I have ever played that wouldn’t work on pc or any other console. Sure you can make a bending style fighting game, but nothing with this much emergent gameplay, nothing this immersive, and nothing like Rumble, where the limit truly is your creativity. Nothing where it truly feels like you’re learning a martial art.

Rumble truly shows why vr is worthwhile, and all the unique strengths this medium brings to the table.

Is the learning curve steep? Yes
Can the lag be painful with peer to peer? Yes
Are some server empty? Always (sorry Australia)
Is it worth it? 100%

Power through it, Rumble has one of the best communities in any game I’ve ever played, just ask any higher belt player and they’ll show you the ropes. We want you all to have fun and stick around. So I hope you’ll join our awesome community, learn, and maybe become a teacher yourself.
Enjoy <3
Your moves got to be precise and sometimes they do not work running in the game is bad  can't refund the game cuz meta is being annoying

I've played Rumble VR a ton on PC before it came to standalone and its one of the most enjoyable and rewarding games ive played in a very long time. You truly get to feel like an earthbending martial artist learning new techniques from an ever expanded roster in a world full of masters ready to teach you, and its all authentic and done by the players. 

Best advice I can give to anyone new is to join the official community wherever or talk to anyone who looks experienced. Everyone wants to help you and being taught by randoms how to do cool stuff is THE Rumble experience.

See you in the ring.
-n

I have played this game for years. You can trust that I know what I'm saying.

THE MOVES WORK PERFECTLY. Many beginners quit because they think they are doing the poses right when they are not. Most of the time it's because they don't realize that THE ROTATION OF YOUR HANDS MATTERS A LOT. You can get the position spot on but have your hand rotated by 90 degrees and not actually hit the pose.

The other main reason beginners quit is that they are not prepared to put effort into the game. THIS GAME IS HARD. It takes TENS OF HOURS OF TRAINING to become relatively comfortable. THERE IS ALWAYS A HARDER MOVE to perform and there is always a person better than you. THE FUN PART IS HAVING YOUR HARD WORK PAY OFF. If you want to play an idle game, play an idle game.
Many people can't come to terms with the fact that they have a ling way to go.

reviews.review_title: Fun all the way to the top.
reviews.review_description: Such an amazing game which I could not recommend more. The gameplay feels amazing and every time you discover and master a new move or a combination of moves you will feel a rush of dopamine that hits every single time. The skill ceiling is genuinely one of the highest of any game due to the technical and creative nature, and learning it is easy and fun due to the fabulous community that is so incredibly friendly and welcoming. A great tip for beginners is to make sure to put your hands in the ready position before performing a summon move, this will make moves feel more consistent. Remember sometimes slow is fast!

When I was playing my regular rumble session, a robber broke into my abode of which I was completely unaware of. As I was kicking some guys butt i threw a punch simultaneously winning the match and knocking the robber out cold. 5 stars

Rumble's a very active game, and the skill floor is probably 8 feet off the ground. However, almost everyone you fight against will help you pull yourself up to that floor, and will show you new ways to use your moves. The community is friendly enough that you can generally just ask for help from your opponent, and they'll teach you to the best of their ability.

tldr: buy rock game, make friends, hit those friends with rocks

No I’m not a clanker. This game is seriously fun and has a pretty good community. You’ll need up having fun and sweating too. It has a big learning curve tho but once you get it, you GET it. 
The only issues I have is the Host-Client thing. When you’re a host, it’s amazing, but as the client it’s horrible. With the new wave of us quest players I hope you get enough funding for servers😭🙏

This is the hardest game I've ever played your movements have to be nearly perfect 

its also one of the most fun games I've played

 the only bad part is sometimes it takes awhile to matchmake but that rarely happens and you can fix it by just changing where you're looking for players or what type of player

 and it doesn't take long to get decent (as long as you don't give up at the start) 

it's also the best gaming community ever the good players help the new and struggling ones and good sportsmanship (giving fist bumps at the end) gives you in game money.

reviews.review_title: The id part of the phone is broken.
reviews.review_description: The id is the only thing that is broken.                                                             It is challenging in pvp but is really fun even if you lose. Learning moves is easy if you look at hand rotation!. Can't wait to get back on and get hit by a  rock that is 80 miles per hour.

Where do I even start, when I first saw that this game existed I wanted a Vr so bad but when I got my quest 2. 3 years ago I later found out it was only on pc vr as a Avatr the last airbender fan all I wanted was an amazing earth bending game to play considering my favorite element is earth. Knowing that I’m finally able to play the one game that I’ve always wanted to play is exciting to me, and I’m now able to become an earthbender. I also love how the ranking system work in this game with the belts and the ranking names, and moves. You can go from an unskilled common earthbender, to a Toph level threat. This game is overall what I’m in to  and I’m am forever grateful that you all created this game.

once you've mastered the basics and become a blackbelt im sorry to say that you just wont find anything quite like Rumble. Yep, there's a steep learning curve at first to test you, but are rewarded with hundreds and thousands of hours of non-stop rock on rock action.

reviews.review_title: One of the best VR games
reviews.review_description: It might feel a bit boring and repetitive on the first couple of levels. The game has a step learning curve. But the sense of flow later in the game completely overweights all the struggle! This is something VR was created for. The game is brilliant
I love getting hit by rocks, get the game more players
I love it.its so unique and the backstory behind all the combos are insane.

reviews.review_title: This game is one of the worst ive played
reviews.review_description: I tryed to have fun but I can't do anything in the game

I really wanted to like playing this game, but it just didn't work for me.
First thing into the game, and the image in my right eye was offset, causing a noticeable black line to appear throughout ALL of my gameplay
after trying to solve the issue, including a reinstall, I ignored it to actually play the game

which was also hard, as both my hands were a full hands length back from my real hands
recalibration after recalibration did nothing to help at all
eventually, I got one arm to match my real arm's length

afterwards, I got used to the actual combat and attacks, but sprinting is closer to rubbing your arms against your chest, rather than a running motion, and walking is slower than a snail.

I ended up beating the tutorial bot, before simply giving up on trying to play
I wish it worked for me, but for $25, these issues were too much to ignore
I’m so grateful to you guys for bringing this to stand alone. I have it on steam as well and I’ve enjoyed playing it there. Yes there is a learning curve, but that’s the beauty of gaming.

Honestly, this game is a gamechanger. The mechanics in RUMBLE have such a high skill ceiling that you can spend years learning and finding out new stuff. And the variety of how you can play is genuinely awesome. 

Definitely recommend

I watched stuff about this game for so long and it was worth it because this game is so much fun and I never even watched avatar BUT there are problems 1 this game is not 5 stars  because people quit to early and say that the game is broken because they can’t get the poses right in three tries don’t be fooled  there uggos who’s brain is smoother than a koalas(look up Tyson chicken breast then look up koala brain)2once  you get to black belt and in the great words of hornet hollow knight “git Gud”fight people way above you because “hey you’re good with about 200 battle points here fight someone way above you who has about 2 3000 battle points and has a tighter fit then you will ever have oh and they finna fly” oh yeah also do get discouraged

Rumble is 100% one of the best VR games I have every played, although it seems difficult at first, it's really rewarding to master the moves, and is really fun to play.

The character customisation is really good, it has a full try-on mode where you can test every cosmetic without having to buy it.

It also has by far the best community of any game I have ever played, barely anyone is toxic, and the rest of the community is really helpful and is willing to help you.

10/10 BUY IT NOW

reviews.review_title: What a joy to learn to yeet rocks
reviews.review_description: This is a fighting game. A pose-based fighting game. It is like no other game I’ve seen. There is quite a learning curve, but that’s the best part! There is a long existing community that is more than willing to help you learn. You can go from just simply punching rocks at people to launching them at high speeds, and if you become good enough, flying with rocks. This is a game that I enjoy just hanging out in the hub area practicing my moves. Join the Buckethead community discord; it’s worth it!

this game was fun i played it and i learned to love like an earth bender! But i will say it was hard when i tried to make the moves and it was good exercise but i’m not gonna let that discourage me ima keep on working hard to become one of the best earthbenders!!!

New player tip #1: Leave your hands open. They will close when the pose is correct so you can see how much wiggle room there is and learn to do things efficiently.

Great game with a VERY high skill ceiling and a the best community I have ever seen in a game (10 matches, all helpful/kind people) likely due to the lack of win:loss records (brilliant choice devs!). You will be bad and get beaten by masters when you start but they will show you tricks and it is super satisfying to figure new stuff out, practice it, and then use it in a match. Rock physics feel great.

Framerate is stable 90.

reviews.review_title: Rumble is what VR was meant to be
reviews.review_description:
I’ve been waiting a long time for this one to finally come to Quest and it doesn’t disappoint!

If you’ve never seen it in action, check out Shoeless on YouTube. He literally invented flying in this earth bending game!

reviews.review_title: Love the game, BUT needs servers!
reviews.review_description:
When your not host the game is almost unplayable, need a server to host the matches and process hit registry details. Make it so block spawning happens on the client end so it appears smooth for the user, and replicate it to the server to check, then to the other clients. I guess there’s an exception when there’s a block in the way of spawning and the client didnt catch it and spawned another block anyway. The client can spawn that block, replicate to the server, and the server sees that another block is in the way, and breaks the block that was just spawned, and sends it back to the client. I know there’s a lot more involved but that’s the basic idea. The game just needs servers if it’s ever going to take off.
I love this game it’s very great and I feel bad giving it 4 stars but the host based matches gotta go

reviews.review_title: The greatest Tutorial is the community
reviews.review_description:
The community is hands down the most sweet kind and caring I’ve ever encountered 

On my first ever match years ago the first thing my opponent did when they played against me was teach me how to improve

It wasn’t in a condescending manner but a caring and respectful manner 

Iron sharpens iron and in Rumble it’s no different 

Great tutorials for those who want to learn on their own is definitely the “rumble pose guide 101” online 

It even teaches combos that are useful

This game had so many eyes on it, millions of views and a clear audience of people wanting to try it out, despite that the quest port took multiple years and when it came it came quietly with zero marketing behind it so no one really knows and the player base has decreased a lot.

There’s high demand for bending vr games we can see that with elements divided so you guys should put more effort into making this game well known and adding more gamemodes like 2v2 to make players go back on the game

This games too good to be having so little players

Literally the best game ever for earth bending this game is quite hard at first but when you practice with Howard 😎 you start to get the jist of it, this game is one of the best games I play  you should def buy this and w devs and the people I battle with￼, our super friendly so  yea it’s a great game overall it’s amazing you guys cooked on this massive W

reviews.review_title: dont waste your money and time
reviews.review_description: This game is horrible. First of the game rearly registers your movement ,and because of that its really hard to do any of your moves you have. Second thing. When ever you go into a game you will always get beaten because people spamming the same move without stopping. And btw lets not talk about the “tutorial”

First couple of lobby’s I joined had people calling me a slur all because I used a defense the whole match because they spammed one move, 

not saying it’s the whole community but obviously people recording for tictok or YouTube won’t put that in a video so take what you read with a grain of salt.

This game shows that fun vr games can still be difficult without being impossible. Absolutely peak, I would've paid $50 for a game like this but it's only $25, would recommend to ANYONE looking for a good vr game.
The most physically demanding but satisfying game on the market. It's absolutely wild how much fun this game is once you get past the learning curve. Stick with it and you'll definitely be rewarded.

I’ve been waiting for this game for around 2 years now and i’ll tell you i am NOT disappointed with the purchase at all! Great game overall 5 star review
Will play this alot

I have no words for how long I’ve been waiting for this game to come to quest(which is four years). I say it’s worth the £20.99. But it is hard to learn the moves and there are people who know how to rocket a disc at you, but you can get the hang of it and learn the game. 
What I think they should add:
-story mode
-custom move builder (once you’ve gotten to the top rank)
-add an earthbending ability that makes the earth go black and white like that one time toph did in Avatar the last airbender
I hugely recommend the game AND the show. Both worth it.

This game is great. The only issue is that almost no one owns the game. Since this game is fully multiplayer, it’s basically unplayable. So I try to refund the game, literally right after buying and playing for less than 20 minutes, and it doesn’t refund properly.

reviews.review_title: Best VR game on the market
reviews.review_description: Steep learning curve but very satisfying to learn. And with a community based around helping others, I believe this is the best VR game you can buy.

reviews.review_title: Near perfect game with a strong future
reviews.review_description: Steep learning curve but such a strong foundation and I'm excited to see how the new Quest players revitalize the playerbase. THIS is what VR was made for. A must buy!!

I played Elements Divided, but it doesn’t even compare to this. The fact they use no buttons and move around your hands and martial art arts poses is so much fun.
There’s so much depths to it too. You can combined moves to make different ones.🤩 I love it ￼
These so-called tutorial doesn’t teach you anything ￼

reviews.review_title: High Skill Floor, but worth it!
reviews.review_description:
Rumble is pretty hard compared to other vr games. The poses you do to earthbend are really strict, but once you learn them they work 100% of the time. 
It also gets much easier when other rumble players teach you how to use the moves effectively, which happens really often because the community is so nice.

Me, Reuben Kelly, tinyhardcoreraf and christion chatman agrees
Side note, im seeing alot of bad reviews on this game mainly revolving around a bad community or moves not registering, the moves are perfectly fine so any issue around those is classed a skill issue and the community is litterally the best one I've seen so maybe just requeue rather than fight the one toxic guy 50 times

I've only had this game for a week now but it is already my favourite game. The game tho, is really hard so if your easy to get angry, dont get this but it isn't like an impossible challenge, its really fun as you get better and better the more you train your muscle memory.

Everytime I do the calibration my arms are way way way too short and It has costed me multiple matches because I can't hit anything away because my arms don't reach, even if I do the poses perfectly nothing happens because my in game arms are not aligned with my real arms! Such a great game that doesn't work

reviews.review_title: What a game it's worth it
reviews.review_description: This is just a inaainly good game I've been wqtliting for it to come to quest for a few years now and there are just so many nice people.
the game works fine, anyone that says otherwise is legitimately just bad ✌️

Where to start? First, it took me 30 minutes just to find settings to adjust in-game, interactions are clunky and awkward, there's no tutorial to help with anything, and the one already there is confusing rather than helpful. Even the UI interaction is clunky and feels awful to interact with.

Matchmaking me instantly with a pro player makes the game unfair from the get-go just for the server to disconnect in the middle of the match.

I genuinely never played any game on vr that made me hate every minute on it more than this game, wasted my time for it to be eligible for a refund.


I would give it negative 3 outta 5 if I could for being such an anti-beginner-friendly game.

reviews.review_title: 30 minutes to throw rocks get tired
reviews.review_description:
The community is horrible. The worst community of any game I’ve ever played. The game is boring takes too long to play a game. Everyone just hides such a disappointment. 
￼
Don't worry about the difficulty. People will teach you. watch shoeless, or better yet just play rumble.

I’ve played this game a bit so far and in a game encountered someone fly on a rock. Love the game and the skill ceiling seems so high and there’s so much emergent gameplay.

10/10
Greatest game I have never played

I got this game because I thought it would be really cool to be an earth bender but it was actually horrible I played for an hour and I’m not gonna play anymore because I want to refund but I’ve already got all. I need to know. there’s this little ghost guy who helps you use your abilities, but for some reason, it’s impossible to use your abilities without the ghost guy and on top of that you only have three abilities and I really don’t know how you unlock a new ones and besides, it’s not like I’d be able to use any other abilities, except one at a time because for some reason, the ghost guy is necessary if you want to use moves because it is so precise and so time sensitive, and I don’t even think it works unless it’s down to the exact molecule that it wants you to be this game needs a heavy amount of polishing to be good and is just a waste of your money ￼￼￼￼
Really fun and good game is wish there would be more things to do but it is a really fun grind game and the community is the best I've seen in a while.
I have been waiting for this game to come out on Meta quest for two years and it finally came out and it is way better than I thought it would be. ￼ I just wish it was more popular.

reviews.review_title: If you love avatar you will love this
reviews.review_description: Best game i ever played the poses are so fun to do but always remember to have water when playing this game
Anyone complaining about the tracking being too precise is just bad.
Not beginner friendly

This game is a very fun game one of my favorites so far but this game is VERY DEMANDING you have to do very specific poses but once you get them it is very easy to do I would 100% get this game the people I met so far are pretty nice and help you most of the time.
As someone with a broken grip I am happy that when you get the right pose it automatically does a fist
GOATED game ten out of ten would recommend
